guide.name: Component Guide
guide.notes: Reference for available section components in the IntegrateWise docs.

sections.title: What Is IntegrateWise
sections.introduction: IntegrateWise maintains organizational continuity. It connects the tools where work already happens, stores truth and context in the Adaptive Spine, and exposes them through the Knowledge Workspace and the Twin.

content.title: The Origin
content.text:
The Spine was born on the floor of Salesforce, where Nirmal spent 13 years as a MuleSoft Integration Architect. Every day, the same ritual: open one system, check another, cross-reference a third, mentally stitch together the picture. The tools were excellent. None of them talked to each other. And someone had to bridge the gap.

That someone was the Human API. That someone built the Spine.

The Spine came first. Everything else followed.

content.title: What It Is
content.text:
IntegrateWise is the system that understands, remembers, reasons, and prepares execution — while execution happens in the customer's own environment.

We do not compete with workflow engines or automation platforms. We compete with the chaos of fragmented tools, disconnected context, and blind operational decisions.

The wedge: Every enterprise has 15+ tools. None of them talk to each other. The human operator becomes the integration layer. IntegrateWise replaces that burden with a unified intelligence layer that connects, unifies, understands, remembers, reasons, governs, prepares, and hands off.
Five Customer Surfaces

IntegrateWise exposes five customer surfaces:

• L1 Workbench — Main domain-specific operational surface
• L2 Cognitive Overlay — Always-available intelligence layer (signals, insights, context)
• L3 Twin Workbench — Conversational reasoning and planning (proposes only)
• L4 Memory Workbench — Knowledge substrate (search, memory, documents)
• Approval Center — Governance surface (review, approve, dispatch — the HARD GATE)

L5 (Operations) and L6 (Execution) are Customer Zero only. Customers receive a Handoff Layer instead of native execution.

content.title: Two Modes
content.text:
Customer Zero Mode: Full stack (L0-L7) for internal ops and demos.

External Customer Mode: Trimmed to 5 surfaces + Handoff (L1 Workbench, L2 Cognitive Overlay, L3 Twin Workbench, L4 Memory Workbench, Approval Center).

content.title: How It Works
content.text:
The operating loop is simple and repeatable:

Connect → Contextualize → Think → Approve → Handoff → Learn

1. Connect — You connect your tools. Data enters the Adaptive Spine via the Continuity Bridge (MCP primary).
2. Contextualize — Unstructured context is linked to entities in the Spine.
3. Think — The Twin reads the full picture and generates proposals with evidence. The Twin proposes only.
4. Approve — You review in the Approval Center. Your decision teaches the system.
5. Handoff — On approval, the system prepares the canonical contract and dispatches to your execution environment.
6. Learn — Outcomes feed back into the Adaptive Spine and Digital Memory.

content.title: Adaptive Spine
content.text:
The Adaptive Spine is the foundational layer of the Knowledge Workspace.

It connects source systems via the Continuity Bridge (MCP primary), extracts truth and relevant context, organizes them into one usable structure, and stores the result so the Knowledge Workspace (and the Twin) can read from one grounded view.

The Adaptive Spine is adaptive by design. It starts with a universal foundation — accounts, contacts, goals, outcomes — and extends itself based on your industry and department.

sections.title: The Turning Point
sections.introduction: How fragmented work led to the creation of the Adaptive Spine and Knowledge Workspace.

content.text:
For years, organizations have been forced to live with a fundamental mismatch: powerful specialized tools that cannot talk to each other, and humans acting as the glue.

This created the Human API problem at massive scale.

IntegrateWise was built to eliminate that burden.

content.title: Key Realization
content.text: The problem was never the individual tools. The problem was the missing layer that maintains continuity across them.

sections.title: What the Spine Is
sections.introduction: The Adaptive Spine is foundational infrastructure that powers the Knowledge Workspace.

content.title: Core Idea
content.text:
The Adaptive Spine is the single source of truth for your organization's entities, relationships, and context.

It is invisible to end users but powers every surface: the Workbench, the Cognitive Overlay, the Twin, Memory, and the Approval Center.

MCP is the primary protocol boundary. The Continuity Bridge is how the Twin and external AIs reach grounded context without ever reading your tools directly.

The Knowledge Workspace (L1) is where operational work happens. Domain views converge on Entity 360, giving you a complete picture of every account, opportunity, ticket, or custom entity — with truth from every connected system and context from the Twin and memory.

sections.title: The Twin
sections.introduction: Bounded reasoning that proposes. You decide.

content.text:
The Twin reads exclusively through the Continuity Bridge over prepared context from the Adaptive Spine and Digital Memory. It never has direct access to your tools.

It generates proposals, evidence, and plans. Approval always happens in the Approval Center (HARD GATE).

content.title: Twin Rule
content.text: Twin proposes only. Humans (or governed processes) approve and act.

sections.title: Connectors Overview
sections.introduction: Connectors are how your tools feed the Adaptive Spine via the Continuity Bridge (MCP primary).

content.text: Every input to the Knowledge Workspace enters through a connector. The Spine doesn't care where data comes from — it cares about what it means and how it relates to your entities.

sections.title: Solutions Overview
sections.introduction: Role-specific expressions of the same Knowledge Workspace.

content.text: Solutions for Account Success, Sales Ops, Business Ops, Finance Ops, and Personal Ops all run on the shared Adaptive Spine, Continuity Bridge, and surfaces. Only the lens and primary workflows change.

sections.title: Glossary
sections.introduction: Key terms in the IntegrateWise system.

content.title: Adaptive Spine
content.text: The foundational layer that unifies truth and context from all connected tools.

content.title: Continuity Bridge (MCP primary)
content.text: The protocol boundary through which the Twin and external AIs access context. No direct tool reads.

content.title: Handoff Layer
content.text: The mechanism that prepares the canonical contract and delivers approved work to the customer's own execution environment.

content.title: HARD GATE
content.text: The Approval Center (L7). No handoff package can be generated without an approval token.

sections.title: Customer Zero Verification Panel
sections.introduction: Observable proof that the system assembles correctly.

content.text:
Each verification box maps to a concrete stage:

Identity → tenant_spine_config (schema_version = v2)
Connectors → connected_connectors (>0)
Hydration → creamy jobs (completed)
Spine → *_data partitions (>0 rows)
Memory → org_memory (>0)
Continuity → assembler (memories returned)
Twin → activation gate (activated = true)

sections.title: Customer Zero Runbook
sections.introduction: 11-step operational playbook for onboarding and validation.

items: Connect first connector (e.g. GitHub or HubSpot)
Confirm Spine data partitions have rows
Activate Twin and confirm proposals appear

content.title: Note
content.text: See the full runbook in the original VitePress reference for timings and failure signatures.
